opportunities.rationale: Highest-conviction pair in the current regime. LLY continues to execute in obesity drugs while NVO has repriced lower on weaker 2026 guidance and trial disappointment. Quality-vs-reset dynamic is intact; the pair should be relatively insulated from index direction in a high-volatility environment. Tightened risk to 2% given elevated VIX.

Costco’s premium membership model and recent beat potential should allow outperformance vs Walmart in a defensive consumer environment. The pair benefits from a shift toward higher-quality retail while keeping beta lower than a pure long discretionary trade.

Long Energy / short Airlines expresses the oil-shock theme cleanly: higher crude prices support XOM’s earnings while pressuring fuel-cost-sensitive carriers like Alaska Air. Exit on confirmed Strait of Hormuz ceasefire or a sharp OPEC supply response that normalises oil prices.

In risk-off, high-volatility regimes BTC tends to outperform high-beta L1s like SOL. This pair isolates that relative dynamic: long BTC as the crypto safe-haven vs short SOL as a higher-beta alt, sized small given crypto volatility and macro cross-currents.

Both names were hit by AI capex concerns, but Amazon’s ~9% drop in early Feb was driven by an aggressive multi-year capex guide while Microsoft’s selloff reflected more moderate expectations. Long MSFT / short AMZN expresses a view that Azure execution and diversified earnings will be rewarded relative to Amazon’s more controversial spending path.

Upgraded to high conviction in light of the Iran war and oil shock. United Airlines is highly exposed to jet fuel costs and geopolitical travel disruptions, while ExxonMobil benefits directly from higher crude realisations. Long XOM / short UAL captures this divergence with reduced index beta; thesis is invalidated by a durable ceasefire or rapid normalisation in oil prices.
